questions.question: Explain the concept of sustainability and its importance in modern engineering practices.
keyPoints: Definition of sustainability as meeting present needs without compromising future generations
Importance in engineering: responsible technology development
Consideration of long-term environmental, social, and economic impacts
Role in creating sustainable products and systems

questions.question: Define Green Computing and list any four practices associated with it.
keyPoints: Green Computing: practice of using computers and IT resources efficiently with minimal environmental impact
Practices include: virtualization, power management, energy-efficient hardware, cloud computing, recycling e-waste, and using renewable energy
Helps reduce carbon footprint of IT operations

questions.question: Explain the Triple Bottom Line concept with examples of each pillar.
keyPoints: Triple Bottom Line: People, Planet, Profit
People: Social equity, fair labor practices, community development
Planet: Environmental protection, reducing emissions, sustainable resource use
Profit: Economic viability, sustainable growth, creating value
Example: A company using solar power (Planet) while providing fair wages (People) and earning profits

questions.question: What is E-waste? Discuss two methods for proper E-waste management.
keyPoints: E-waste: discarded electronic devices containing hazardous materials
Methods: Recycling, Reuse, Refurbishing, Deep Recycling
Recycling: mechanical separation and material recovery
Deep Recycling: chemical processes to recover precious metals
Proper disposal prevents soil and water pollution

questions.question: Define PUE (Power Usage Effectiveness) and explain how it measures energy efficiency in data centers.
keyPoints: PUE: Power Usage Effectiveness metric for data center efficiency
Formula: PUE = Total Facility Energy / IT Equipment Energy
Ideal PUE is 1.0 (all energy used for computing)
Lower PUE indicates better energy efficiency
Typical values range from 1.1 to 2.0

questions.question: List any four UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) relevant to technology and engineering.
keyPoints: SDG 7: Affordable and Clean Energy
SDG 9: Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure
SDG 12: Responsible Consumption and Production
SDG 13: Climate Action
SDG 17: Partnerships for the Goals

questions.question: Discuss the environmental impact of IT operations and explain how organizations can implement sustainable computing practices.
keyPoints: Environmental impacts: high energy consumption, carbon emissions, e-waste generation, heat generation
Sustainable practices: virtualization, cloud computing, power management, green data centers
E-waste management through proper recycling and disposal
Use of renewable energy sources
Green software development practices
Employee awareness and policies

questions.question: Explain the concept of Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) and its importance in evaluating environmental impact of products.
keyPoints: LCA definition: method to assess environmental impacts throughout product lifecycle
Four stages: raw material extraction, manufacturing, use, disposal
Importance: identifies environmental hotspots, informs design decisions, supports sustainability claims
Benefits: helps reduce environmental impact, aids regulatory compliance, improves brand image

questions.question: Describe the circular economy model and explain how it differs from the linear economy. How can IT companies adopt circular economy principles?
keyPoints: Linear economy: take-make-dispose model leading to resource depletion and waste
Circular economy: reduce-reuse-recycle model emphasizing resource efficiency
Key principles: waste elimination, circulating products and materials, regenerating nature
IT adoption: product-as-a-service, modular design, refurbishing, take-back programs, material recovery
Benefits: reduced e-waste, resource conservation, new business models

questions.question: Discuss the role of Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) in promoting sustainability in the IT industry.
keyPoints: CSR definition and scope in IT industry
Environmental responsibilities: reducing carbon footprint, e-waste management, green computing
Social responsibilities: digital inclusion, skill development, community engagement
Economic responsibilities: sustainable growth, ethical business practices
Benefits: improved brand reputation, stakeholder trust, regulatory compliance, long-term profitability
